  "clarification_question": "Which focus do you want for this paragraph? A) Writing polish and clarity at the sentence/paragraph level (R1.6), B) Checking the internal argument and whether the stated findings are supported by the reported numbers/measures (R1.2 + R1.3), or C) High-level reframing of the contribution/claims without editing sentences (R1.1).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The paragraph presents a causal narrative from observed divergence to a proposed mechanism and fix, so checking the internal logic is central.",
    "R1.3": "Multiple quantitative claims (e.g., 62% ODR reduction, BLEU variance changes) invite verification that conclusions match the reported evidence in the text.",
    "R1.6": "A request to 'take a look at this paragraph' commonly implies expectations around readability, precision, and tone at the sentence level.",
    "RX.1": "Any assessment should disclose uncertainty where the paragraph lacks full methodological detail.",
    "RX.2": "Feedback should avoid endorsing or amplifying overclaims (e.g., 'near-deterministic') beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "The task must remain a projection of responsibilities without performing edits or additional analyses.",
    "RX.4": "Guidance should avoid encouraging risky operational changes or overconfident deployment claims based solely on this paragraph.",
    "RX.5": "Any evaluative points should be traceable to specific sentences or claims within the provided text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus do you want for this paragraph? A) Writing polish and clarity at the sentence/paragraph level (R1.6), B) Checking the internal argument and whether the stated findings are supported by the reported numbers/measures (R1.2 + R1.3), or C) High-level reframing of the contribution/claims without editing sentences (R1.1).

rationale.R1.2: The paragraph presents a causal narrative from observed divergence to a proposed mechanism and fix, so checking the internal logic is central.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple quantitative claims (e.g., 62% ODR reduction, BLEU variance changes) invite verification that conclusions match the reported evidence in the text.
rationale.R1.6: A request to 'take a look at this paragraph' commonly implies expectations around readability, precision, and tone at the sentence level.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessment should disclose uncertainty where the paragraph lacks full methodological detail.
rationale.RX.2: Feedback should avoid endorsing or amplifying overclaims (e.g., 'near-deterministic') beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: The task must remain a projection of responsibilities without performing edits or additional analyses.
rationale.RX.4: Guidance should avoid encouraging risky operational changes or overconfident deployment claims based solely on this paragraph.
rationale.RX.5: Any evaluative points should be traceable to specific sentences or claims within the provided text.
